evals.query: In my Kora service, consume plain String messages from the topic configured at kafka.consumer.userEvents and log each value.
expected_behavior: Annotates a method on a @Component class with @KafkaListener("kafka.consumer.userEvents")
Uses the void process(String value) signature for simple value consumption
Adds KafkaModule to the @KoraApp interface via extends (not implements)
Provides application.conf with kafka.consumer.userEvents.topics and driverProperties bootstrap.servers
Does not use field injection or @Autowired-style wiring; relies on Kora constructor injection

evals.query: Deserialize incoming Kafka messages into a UserEvent record with userId and eventType fields using Kora JSON.
expected_behavior: Declares the record annotated with @Json
Annotates the @KafkaListener method parameter with @Json (e.g. void process(@Json UserEvent event))
Adds the ru.tinkoff.kora:json-module dependency and JsonModule to the @KoraApp interface
Does not register a manual Jackson Deserializer class in driverProperties for the JSON value

evals.query: Make my JSON Kafka listener resilient: when a message fails to deserialize, log it and continue instead of crashing the consumer.
expected_behavior: Uses the nullable signature void process(@Nullable @Json Event event, @Nullable Exception exception)
Checks exception != null before processing the event
Explains that an uncaught exception causes Kora to restart the consumer
Optionally mentions RecordValueDeserializationException and getRecord() to retrieve the raw ConsumerRecord<byte[], byte[]>
Does not invent a @KoraTransaction annotation or a separate error-handler component

evals.query: Skip messages whose userId is null or blank in my Kafka listener, while still counting them in metrics.
expected_behavior: Throws KafkaSkipRecordException imported from ru.tinkoff.kora.kafka.common.exceptions
Passes the underlying cause to the KafkaSkipRecordException constructor
Notes that skipping commits the offset and continues to the next record
Does not throw a plain RuntimeException expecting the consumer to silently skip

evals.query: I need exactly-once-style processing: only commit the Kafka offset after the database write succeeds.
expected_behavior: Adds a Consumer<K, V> parameter to the @KafkaListener method to switch to manual commit
Calls consumer.commitSync() only after the business logic succeeds
Does not commit on exception so the record is reprocessed
Does not rely on enable.auto.commit for the commit-after-write guarantee

evals.query: Process Kafka records in batches for higher throughput and log the batch size.
expected_behavior: Uses the ConsumerRecords<K, V> parameter signature so the whole poll batch is delivered
Iterates over records and uses records.count() for the batch size
Notes that commitSync() is called automatically after the whole batch
May tune max.poll.records / fetch.min.bytes in driverProperties
Does not fall back to a single-record signature when batch throughput is requested

evals.query: Commit offsets and clear a local cache when partitions are revoked during a Kafka rebalance.
expected_behavior: Implements ru.tinkoff.kora.kafka.common.consumer.ConsumerAwareRebalanceListener as a @Component
Annotates the rebalance listener with @Tag matching the listener's tag class
Commits in onPartitionsRevoked via consumer.commitSync() and avoids committing in onPartitionsLost
Does not use the raw Kafka ConsumerRebalanceListener or @Qualifier; uses Kora @Tag

evals.query: One consumer must load-balance work across instances, another must broadcast every message to all instances. How do I configure both in Kora?
expected_behavior: Configures the load-balanced consumer with a group.id in driverProperties (subscribe strategy)
Configures the broadcast consumer without a group.id (assign strategy) and notes only one topic is allowed
Keeps both under kafka.consumer.<name> with topics and bootstrap.servers
Does not claim group.id is required for the broadcast/assign consumer

evals.query: Write an integration test for my Kafka listener that spins up a real Kafka broker.
expected_behavior: Uses @KoraAppTest(Application.class) and injects the listener with @TestComponent
Starts Kafka with Testcontainers and overrides bootstrap.servers via KoraConfigModification / KoraAppTestConfigModifier
Asserts asynchronously with Awaitility against state the listener records
Does not mock the @KafkaListener method; uses a real broker via Testcontainers

evals.query: After consuming a record I want to publish two messages atomically so consumers only see them if both succeed.
expected_behavior:
Declares a @KafkaPublisher interface extending TransactionalPublisher

for the transactional producer
Sends both messages inside publisher.inTx(producer -> { ... }) so they commit or abort together
Configures idPrefix / maxPoolSize / maxWaitTime for the transactional publisher and read_committed isolation on the consumer
Does not use a non-existent @KoraTransaction annotation or KafkaTransactionContext consumer parameter
